  • A new Grasshopper Direct presentation takes place next week, featuring Romeo is a Dead Man - It's the third presentation from Suda51's Grasshopper Manufacture studio. 5pm BST / 8PM EST December 4th

Free Games / Subscription

  • December is getting five (more than the usual 3) PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games - Lego Horizon Adventures, The Outlast Trials, Killing Floor 3, Neon White and Synduality: Echo of Ada. Available December 2nd

Business

  • Cyberpunk 2077 has now sold 35 million copies, hitting the milestone faster than The Witcher 3 did
  • Coatsink to lay off half its staff due to "challenging co-development market" - up to 60 roles affected
  • Splash Damage places entire studio into consultation ahead of redundancies - All staff members affected, after UK studio stalwart was purchased by private equity in September

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-26
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Tekken boss Harada says he’s ‘quite interested’ in Capcom’s decision to charge for Street Fighter tournament streams - says the big money of fighting tournaments isn't reflected in game revenue
  • Roblox boss says problem of predators is an "opportunity" for the platform, in disastrous interview following latest child safety measures
  • Yet another Nier Automata crossover has been announced, this time in Final Fantasy 7 Ever Crisis

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Classics Update for Switch: Battletoads (NES), Ninja Gaiden II: The Dark Sword of Chaos (NES), Bionic Commando (GB) and Kid Icarus: Of Myths and Monsters (GB)

Business

  • Embracer Group divests Arc Games and Cryptic Studios, while several key rights are retained within the group

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-25
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The Steam Awards 2025 nominations are active
  • Far Cry Getting TV Adaptation From Rob Mac and Alien Earth Creator Noah Hawley
  • Assassin's Creed Shadows Attack on Titan Crossover Is Now Available — But Free Quest Only Available For a Month

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-24
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Call of Duty, OD Actor, Udo Kier, has passed away
  • Sandfall Interactive and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 sweep Golden Joystick Awards 2025 - French developer won seven awards, including Studio of the Year and Game of the Year
  • Don't forget to vote in The Game Awards
  • Fan game TimeSplitters Rewind releases after a 13 year wait
  • FBC: Firebreak's Rogue Protocol update is delayed to January as it "still needs a bit more time"

Business

  • Ubisoft's Tencent deal goes through - The French publisher confirms a €1.16 billion investment in Vantage Studios for 26.32% stake
  • Ubisoft announces H1 earnings

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-21
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Pokémon TCG Pocket wins Google Play's Best Game of the Year award
  • Great Ormond Street Hospital is looking for a Gamer in Residence - Role aims to improve patient care, minimise trauma, and ensure that every young person has someone to game with
  • Silent Hill 2 Remake surprise-launches on Xbox Series S/X
  • Eminem will hire Agent 47 to kill Slim Shady in Hitman’s next Elusive Target mission - Releases December 1st
  • Vampire Crawlers, a first-person deck building Vampire Survivors spin-off, is coming next year - Vampire Crawlers is coming in 2026 to Xbox Series S/X, PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC, and will be available day one with Xbox Game Pass. A sequel to the original game is also in development
  • New 007 First Light trailer confirms Bond’s car is an Aston Martin Valhalla
  • Dave the Diver is finally on Xbox, and it’s available now
  • BAFTA Games Concert confirms UK city dates and adds Fallout, Returnal, and Helldivers

Business

  • Ubisoft confirms €1.16 billion Tencent investment in new subsidiary is 'imminent'
  • Unity signs 'multi-title development partnership' with 2K Games
  • Resident Evil Survival Unit tops 1 million downloads in 24 hours

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-20
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Fortnite Chapter 6 Finale Event Trailer Teases Godzilla and Kill Bill Appearances as Chapter Seven Looms - Live event takes place November 29 at 11 a.m. PT / 2 p.m. ET
  • Epic and Unity announce ‘unique’ partnership - Unity Ddevelopers will be able to publish their games into Fortnite from next year

Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving PlayStation Plus soon

Business

  • Dispatch has sold two million copies
  • Just Cause Dev Avalanche Closes Liverpool Studio And Lays Off Workers - 31 developers are impacted
  • Dragon's Dogma 2 sells 4m copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-19
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Valve accidentally ruins a game's (Planet Centauri) sales again after apologizing for ruining its sales the first time
  • ‘I don’t think we qualify’: Megabonk dev withdraws nomination from The Game Awards. Developer claims Megabonk doesn't count as a "debut" indie game as “I’ve made games in the past under different studio names, so Megabonk is not my debut game”
  • Nier Director Yoko Taro assures players he’s been working on projects, but they keep getting cancelled

Free Games / Subscription

  • More Xbox Game Pass Games Coming

Business

  • Bungie settles Destiny 2 copyright lawsuit with writer in undisclosed settlement. If no further objections are made within 60 days, the court will presume "all parties have firmly agreed upon a compromise"
  • Farming Simulator 25 surpasses four million copies sold
  • Riot sells Hytale IP and Hypixel brand back to original co-founder

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-18
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Rebecca Heineman, creator of Bard’s Tale 3, Dragon Wars and the person who ported Doom to the 3DO, has passed
  • The Game Awards 2025 Nominees Announced - Game of the Year is going to be either Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, Death Stranding 2, Donkey Kong Bananza, Hades II, Hollow Knight: Silksong or Kingdom Come: Deliverance II
  • Xbox has announced a Partner Preview showcase for later this week - Thursday, November 20 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6m GMT
  • Helldivers studio CEO says people ‘jump to extreme takes’ when debating AI
  • The first official photos from The Legend of Zelda live-action movie have been published

Business

  • Paradox Interactive parts ways with Cities: Skylines studio after over 15 years
  • Improbable founder Herman Narula (who threatened to leave the UK over tax plans for wealth creators) has seen cumulative losses (almost £425 million) at his SoftBank-backed company

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-17
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Rebecca Heineman, creator of Bard’s Tale 3, Dragon Wars, has been diagnosed with adenocarcinoma and is in a fight for her life
  • Roblox rolls out next phase of IP licensing platform - Any eligible IP holder can offer content for use on Roblox after platform debuted with a select number of partners earlier this year
  • Over 200 Rockstar North staff sign letter demanding "immediate reinstatement" of fired colleagues - Independent Workers' Union of Great Britain says letter has been handed to senior management at developer

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-14
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Red Dead Redemption is officially coming to Switch 2, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and mobile next month. Netflix subscribers will also be able to download the mobile versions as part of their subscription
  • Fallout Season 2’s full trailer is out now, as Fallout 76 becomes free via Amazon Prime - Series returns December 17th
  • Kojima Productions has announced Death Stranding Isolations, an animated series coming to Disney+ in 2027 - The new series is seperate from the previously announced animated film Death Stranding Mosquito
  • Silent Hill F update adds ‘Casual’ difficulty and lets players skip part of a New Game+ playthrough

Free Games / Subscription

  • Five classic Blizzard games have been added to Xbox Retro Classics (powered by Antstream) - The Lost Vikings, Rock & Roll Racing, Blackthorne, Lost Vikings 2: Norse by Norsewest, RPM Racing

Business

  • Tomb Raider developer Crystal Dynamics announces yet more layoffs - Around 30 people affected
  • Ubisoft delays H1 FY25-26 results and temporarily halts trading of shares - Delayed results will be issued "in the coming days". Ubisoft's shares have plummeted 95.51% in five years, and are down 49.44% year-on-year
  • PUBG creator Brendan "PlayerUnknown" Greene is planning a new multiplayer FPS. The current plan for it is 100 player vs 100 player fights

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-13
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Valve announces a new Steam ‘console’, controller and VR headset - All releasing early 2026
  • The next Ratchet & Clank game is a free-to-play multiplayer shooter for mobile - Ratchet and Clank: Ranger Rumble
  • Sony and NCSoft Announce Horizon Steel Frontiers, a New Mobile-First MMORPG Set in Guerrilla's Horizon Universe
  • Infinity Nikki’s Infold believes there’s ‘untapped demand’ for non-violent big budget games
  • Nintendo reveals the first trailer for The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, with Brie Larson joining the cast. Bowser Jr will be played by Benny Safdie, the filmmaker and actor who wrote and directed The Smashing Machine and Uncut Gems
  • Valve will soon support Android games on Steam

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PS Plus Catalog games - Drop November 18th

Business

  • Sega is raising Japanese employees’ salaries again, three years after its previous increase - Approx 10% increase
  • 2K, Riot, Epic execs found marketing network Games Growth Guild - "Game developers have solved incredible technical challenges, but marketing and distribution remain the hardest problems"
  • Digiphile is a new charitable discovery platform from a group of Humble Bundle veterans
  • Nexon Q3 results
  • Sharkmob confirms "difficult but necessary" restructuring, unknown number of jobs at risk

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-12
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Elden Ring Nightreign DLC, The Forsaken Hollows, is coming next month - Releases December 4, and will include additional Nightfarers, Bosses, and more
  • An official Death Stranding 2 exoskeleton is being released in collaboration with Kojima Productions
  • PokéPark Kanto, the first permanent Pokémon theme park, will open in February - Opens February 5, 2026
  • ‘We’re here for the tough love’: Skate studio acknowledges that players have been having issues since launch
  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is getting another three free characters; NiGHTS, Super Monkey Ball's AiAi and Tangle/Whisper from a Sonic comic
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls is getting a second closed beta, with Ghost Rider and Spider-Man playable and this time, no interruptions - It'll be available starting the 5th of December, and will run interrupted for 72-hours
  • The Game Awards 2025 to stream on Amazon Prime

Business

  • Unity Third Quarter Financial Results
  • Sony Game & Network Services Financial Results

Rumours

  • Valve expected to make an announcement today; believed to be Steam Frame / Steam Controller announcement

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-11
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Nintendo Direct focused on The Super Mario Galaxy Movie - Wednesday, November 12
  • PlayStation State of Play announced for today; focused on Japan and Asian games (will be broadcast in Japanese with subtitles) - 2pm PT/ 5pm ET / 10pm UK Time. 40 Minutes of information

Business

  • Ghost of Yotei sold 3.3 million copies in a month, Sony says
  • On its 5th birthday, PS5 sales remain virtually neck-and-neck with PS4 - PS5 clears 84 million units; two million behind PS4 at the same stage, going into its sixth holiday season

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-10
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Clair Obscur snubbed in Grammy video game soundtrack nominations - Games nominated in the video game category this year include Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora – Secrets of the Spires (Pinar Toprak), Helldivers 2 (Wilbert Roget), Indiana Jones And The Great Circle (Gordy Haab), Star Wars Outlaws: Wild Card & A Pirate’s Fortune (Cody Matthew Johnson & Wilbert Roget, II), and Sword of the Sea (Austin Wintory)
  • Arc Raiders breaks another concurrent record as player count soars; 400,000
  • FromSoft's parent company reveals we'll get Elden Ring Nightreign DLC by March, Nightreign "performing well beyond initial expectations"
  • Turns out Halo Infinite isn't infinite at all as Halo Studios ends active development after just four years

Business

  • Stalker 2 dev strikes down copycat game Misery with DMCA claim - Misery dev responds by posting a screed of pejorative slurs, prompting a follow-up apology
  • Red Dead Redemption 2 is now the fourth-biggest-selling game of all time, having sold 79 million copies since it debuted in 2018
  • Take-Two Interactive second quarter financial results
  • Roblox Corp sued by State of Texas for allegedly platforming pedophiles

Rumours

  • Silent Hill 2 Remake release date leaks via Microsoft Store (21st November), all but confirming the Xbox version is coming soon

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-07
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Switch 2 version of Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds gets a release date and Switch upgrade path pricing - Thursday, December 4. $10 upgrade from Switch 1 version ($5 for the first week)
  • Yogscast Games announces two new roguelikes, Infinity Sweeper and The Gate Must Stand - Both games will be released in the first half of 2026
  • Ichiban Kasuga from Like a Dragon joins Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds for free, with a themed festival this weekend
  • Square Enix says it wants generative AI to be doing 70% of its QA and debugging by the end of 2027
  • The Game Awards' Future Class program remains on ice

Business

  • Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra has received another delay ‘beyond early 2026’ - Game was previously set to release in 2025, and now no longer has a release window
  • Grand Theft Auto 6 has been delayed again to the end of 2026 - new release date is November 19th, 2026
  • Square Enix announces more layoffs as part of US and Europe restructuring - "nearly all areas" of Square Enix's western business affected by latest cuts, it tells staff. Wording highlights that it is focused on the publishing duties of the western teams. One person affected in Europe said that nearly 140 people were told they were “at risk” in the London office alone
  • Yet another NetEase studio has closed, this time former Watch Dogs devs’ Bad Brain

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-06
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • An official limited-time island based on Disneyland is coming to Fortnite today - The island, which is titled Disneyland Game Rush, launches at 8am PT / 11am ET / 4pm GMT. Players will be able to find it by either searching for its name or using its specific island code, which is 4617-4819-8826. The island will open with a central hub, from which players can visit seven mini-games, each based on a different Disneyland ride
  • Marvel Games says it’ll continue to work with Spider-Man and Wolverine studio Insomniac ‘for many years to come’
  • Nintendo says it plans a ‘consistent release’ of new films following the Super Mario Galaxy and Zelda movies - Nintendo says it is "building a framework" for future movies
  • Football Manager 26 launches on Steam with "mostly negative" reviews

Business

  • Rockstar claims dismissals were due to information leaks, not union busting - Says it "took action against a small number of individuals" for "distributing and discussing confidential information"
  • Krafton posts "record-high cumulative" performance in Q3 2025 - Revenue was up 21% year-on-year to 870.6 billion KRW ($604.2 million), with operating profit also up by 7.5% to 348.8 billion KRW ($241.9 million) across the same period. It took the company's year-to-date operating profit past KRW 1 trillion for the first time
  • Google and Epic strike a deal that could open up Android to third-party stores - the deal would apply globally and could cut Google's fees to as low at 9%
  • Fortnite's Simpsons crossover caused a spike in new players, and tempted back record numbers of returning gamers, too
  • Sumo Group-owned outsourcing studio Atomhawk closes Canadian branch

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-05
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • US patent office director orders re-examination of Nintendo’s patent on summoning characters to make them battle
  • NetEase’s closed studio, T-Minus Zero Entertainment, is back from the dead - name has been acquired by staff. "Reimagined" studio to relaunch as a production company

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass Games for November

Business

  • Sega Sammy's fiscal year ending March 2025
  • Nintendo Financial Results

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-04
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Olympics ends Esports plans with Saudi Arabia after just one year; The IOC says it decided to end the announced partnership following a review
  • The original Saints Row’s design director says he’s been ‘asked to create a pitch’ for a potential prequel

Business

  • Nintendo Switch 2’s phenomenal launch continues as it clears 10.36 million units – more than PS4 and PS5
  • Another NetEase Games studio is shutting down; Fantastic Pixel Castle was founded in 2023 by a Warcraft veteran to create a fantasy MMO
  • Mario Kart World sold 9.57 Million copies in 4 months. Donkey Kong Bananza has sold 3.49 million units
  • Dispatch has sold 1 Million copies in 10 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-11-03
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The Mortuary Assistant dev unveils its next horror project, and its a Paranormal Activity game; Paranormal Activity: Threshold
  • Arc Raider is "committed to skill and fun, not unfair advantages" and is refunding gear lost to cheaters - even if you didn't report them

Business

  • Nintendo wins lawsuit against player who kept streaming pirated Switch games and taunted ‘I can do this all day’ - Jesse Keighin told Nintendo's legal team: "You might run a corporation, I run the streets"
  • Roblox Q3 results

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-31
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Fox Hunt, the online multiplayer mode for Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater, is available now - the 12-player online multiplayer mode offers two different game types
  • Animal Crossing: New Horizons is getting a Nintendo Switch 2 Edition ($5 upgrade) and a huge free update that will add Lego furniture and playable NES and Game Boy games

Business

  • Nintendo has been denied a patent in Japan for a Pokémon-like capturing mechanic
  • Fall Guys veterans launch new studio Panic Stations to make multiplayer co-op games - Planning to ship multiple games per year, recruiting now
  • Heart Machine lays off more staff for the second time this month, two weeks before launch
  • Don't Nod H1 revenues rise to €7m, announces partnership with Netflix - Lost Records: Bloom & Rage "remained under expectations", leading to write-down of €13.1 million
  • Xbox Q1 hardware sales down 29% while third-party content experiences "better-than-expected performance"

Rumours

  • Amazon’s Lord of the Rings MMO has reportedly also been cancelled

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-30
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Resident Evil Requiem is getting a Switch 2 Pro Controller, Amiibo, and coming to Fortnite - Players who purchase Reqiuem through the Epic Game Store will receive Fortnite content
  • Take-Two’s CEO doesn’t think a Grand Theft Auto built with AI would be very good - Strauss Zelnick believes AI's usefor games is limited because it lacks creativity
  • Nintendo is releasing a Zelda Breath of the Wild vinyl box set in the West to gauge the demand for physical soundtracks there - Soundtrack will be released in 2-LP and 8-LP editions. Collaboration with Laced Records
  • ‘We’re still figuring it out’: John Romero hasn’t given up on his axed Xbox shooter - hints that it is about 50% completed and has costed roughly $50 million to develop to this point

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PlayStation Plus Essential games confirmed - Stray, WRC 24 and Totally Accurate Battle Simulator

Business

  • Games London launches new accelerator program to help UK studios develop own IP - The two month training program will help studios develop IP
  • Capcom H1 2025 Results (six months ended September 30, 2025)

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-29
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Amazon ends new content updates for New World: Aeternum following massive layoffs, says 'servers will be live through 2026'
  • Apple has been appointed to The Game Awards’ Advisory Board
  • The original Splinter Cell and Hitman are among 16 new games added to GOG’s Preservation Program

Business

  • MultiVersus directors form new studio (Airlock Games) in response to ‘complexity of AAA games’ - their first title is a 16-bit space horror game
  • Remedy "not satisfied" with Q3 results - Developer records operating loss of €16.4 million, revenue down 32% to €12.2 million
  • Former Lionsgate Games creative director (Amanda Kruse) joins Blumhouse Games

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-28
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Capcom insists it will still charge to watch Street Fighter tournament streams, but is still preparing pricing details - It was previously announced that fans would have to pay up to $40 to watch two tournament finals
  • Forza Motorsport is taking a back seat as Xbox focuses on games releasing sooner, Phil Spencer says - "We understand the reaction many people had when we scaled back Turn 10 studios", the Xbox executive said
  • After 12 years of development, Fan-made Timesplitters Rewind finally has a release date; November 23rd

Business

  • Netflix shutters Squid Game: Unleashed developer Boss Fight Entertainment - The streaming giant recently explained it will be adopting a 'less is more' approach to its video game business
  • Escape From Duckov has reached 2 million units sold in less than 2 weeks

Rumours

  • Amazon plans to cut 30,000 corporate jobs across core departments (including video-games)

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-27
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • GDC lowers ticket prices, streamlines pass types in Festival of Gaming 'reimagination'
  • Halo: Campaign Evolved is coming next year for Xbox Series, PC and PS5. Campaign only remake in Unreal Engine 5. Online feedback has been mixed, with many claiming Microsoft "hired that man" - a jab at the visual identity of the game based on amateur fan "remasters" on YouTube that don't focus on visual cohesion (dubbed the "Nintendo Hire That Man" trend)
  • Fallout 4 is getting an ‘Anniversary Edition’ and coming to Nintendo Switch 2 - Anniversary Edition bundles the 10-year-old RPG with its six official add-ons, in addition to over 150 pieces of ‘Creation Club’ game mods. Will be available digitally on Xbox One, Series S/X, PS4/5, PC and Switch 2 in 2026

Business

  • Roblox's Steal A Brainrot creator sues copycat Fortnite Creative game, Stealing Brainrots - Creator wants actual damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, and a permanent injunction. Steal The Brainrot, another Steal A Brainrot clone also available on Epic's platform, is officially licensed and therefore does not form part of this lawsuit
  • Pokemon TCG Pocket has eclipsed 150 million downloads
  • Pokémon Legends: Z-A sold 5.8 million copies in its first week, making it the fifth best Pokémon launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-24
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Fallout 4 is getting an ‘Anniversary Edition’ and coming to Nintendo Switch 2 - releases November 10 for Xbox One / Series S/X, PS4/5 and Nintendo Switch 2
  • Assassin’s Creed Shadows has been officially confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 - releases December 2nd
  • After numerous delays, Sonic Rumble finally has a release date - PC / mobile November 5th

Business

  • PUBG and Subnautica publisher Krafton says it’s transforming into an ‘AI-first’ company
  • Ubisoft is asking staff at The Division studio Massive Entertainment to volunteer to be laid off
  • Pokémon Legends: Z-A sells 5.8m units during launch week

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-23
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • ‘Generative entertainment company’ Wolf Games has signed a partnership with NBCUniversal, which will see the release of generative AI games based on its film and television IP
  • 2 years after release, Nintendo is updating Pikmin 4 with new content, including a Photo Mode

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass Games for October

Business

  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds Surpasses 1 Million Copies Sold. Player stats include over 74.5 million races, 31.5 million Grand Prix and over 13 million ranked matches
  • Trials studio Ubisoft RedLynx is laying off up to 60 staff as it refocuses on ‘small screens’
  • Gamescom Asia reports record 206,149 visitors

Rumours

  • Xbox is more expensive for developers too, as Microsoft bumps up dev kit cost from $1500 to $2000 - a 30 percent increase in cost. Reason given is "macroeconomic developments"

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-22
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The Sims Mobile to be sunset in January 2026 - Game has been delisted on app stores, will no longer be updated
  • Nintendo’s second Kirby Air Riders Direct is this week, and will last an hour - Sakurai had already presented a 47 minute Kirby Air Riders Direct in August
  • Halo co-creator says he’s ‘disappointed’ he and some former colleagues weren’t credited for their work on Battlefield 6 - Ridgeline Games "built the foundation" before EA closed it last year, Marcus Lehto said

Business

  • Remedy CEO Tero Virtala steps down after nine years - Markus Mäki acts as interim CEO
  • 12 Welsh game studios secure $775,000 in funding to help develop "innovative games and immersive experiences" - Creative Wales' Games Development Fund aims to support devs to "achieve self-sustaining growth"
  • Escape From Duckov waddles into the extraction shooter space with over 200,000 concurrent players
  • Rovio is laying off 36 staff, says Angry Birds Dream Blast has ‘not been performing as expected’

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-21
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Next-gen Xbox confirmed in development, following rumours Microsoft would stop making consoles
  • Sonic Team boss wants more year-long celebrations of Sonic characters, following the Year of Shadow

Business

  • Mediatonic founder launches new group of studios under the banner Skeleton Crew - Skeleton Crew will consist of a slate of new companies, plus the existing teams at Payload, Brightrock and Mythwright
  • Ball X Pit sells 300,000 units in 5 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-20
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Battlefield 6 has become the biggest launch in series history, selling 7 million in three days - Over 172 million matches were played during the game's first weekend
  • Sleeping Dogs movie back on track as Marvel star Simu Liu reveals the draft script is "done"
  • Football Manager 26 will finally include official FIFA licensing for the first time, with a special International Management update for the World Cup

Business

  • ‘I did not walk away’: Former Assassin’s Creed boss says he didn’t resign, he was ‘asked to step aside’ by Ubisoft

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-17
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Quantic Dream’s first multiplayer game is Spellcasters Chronicles, a 3v3 strategy action game
  • McDonald’s Japan is selling Street Fighter themed burgers
  • Marvel Rivals adds PvE Marvel Zombies mode - Starts October 23rd

Business

  • 2K appoints Ben Brinkman as 31st Union studio head - Brinkman to start role on October 20
  • EA confirms Battlefield 6 is the best-selling entry in the series, with over 7m copies sold and 172m online matches played
  • Silent Hill 2 remake shipments and digital sales top 2.5 million - Silent Hill series surpasses 10 million units shipped

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-16
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Ninja Gaiden, Dead or Alive Legend Tomonobu Itagaki Has Passed Away
  • Sonic the Hedgehog has been added as a sidekick on GPS navigation app Waze
  • Capcom ‘took a lot of convincing’ to bring the original Resident Evil games to GOG, exec says - Capcom felt the remakes were the main way to play Resident Evil 1-3 in the modern era

Free Games / Subscription

  • October’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue

Business

  • Sound director Dan Forden, best known as Mortal Kombat’s ‘Toasty’ guy, has retired after 37 years

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-15
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Summer Game Fest confirms 2026 date and venue change - Next year's Summer Games Fest will take place in Hollywood's Dolby Theatre on Friday June 5th 2026
  • Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy is a mobile 3v3 battle game which brings Final Fantasy characters into modern Tokyo - Set for release in 2026

Business

  • Banjo-Kazooie director Gregg Mayles confirms Rare exit after 36 years
  • Following the cancellation of its live service God of War, Sony’s Bluepoint is hiring for a third-person action game
  • Owlcat Games unveils free educational resource hub for game industry professionals
  • Assassin's Creed franchise lead Marc-Alexis Côté leaves Ubisoft following formation of Tencent subsidiary

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-14
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Arrowhead is delaying Helldivers 2 updates to fix its performance problems: 'We're focusing way more on addressing these issues to make sure this sort of thing doesn't happen again'
  • Lego Party and Digital Eclipse’s Tetris Time Warp will soon be available to stream on Netflix - They're two of five streamable party games coming to the service this holiday season

Business

  • NetEase divests Fantastic Pixel Castle as studio hunts for new publisher to avoid closure

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-13
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • New Dissidia Final Fantasy is coming to iOS and Android. More info due October 14th
  • A new ToeJam & Earl game is ‘in the planning stage’, co-creator reveals - movie is currently in search of a director
  • Genshin Impact's new UGC system is almost here – here's what to expect when Miliastra Wonderland launches later this month
  • Mortal Kombat 3 movie confirmed before the release of Mortal Kombat 2

Business

  • Halo art director leaves after 17 years with cryptic message suggesting it was on bad terms - "Stay strong, take evidence when necessary and find where you belong"
  • Remedy issues profit warning with FBC: Firebreak treading water - Remedy said the impairment has no effect on its cash position

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-10
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Mark Cerny triggers PS6 speculation by discussing AMD tech coming to ‘a future console in a few years’ time’
  • Nintendo says its ‘Close to You’ Pikmin videos were Nintendo Pictures short films, rather than a game or movie teaser
  • The Minecraft DLC pack is now available for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds - First of six paid guest DLC packs planned for the game

Business

  • Kimberley Turner and Mark South appointed Double Eleven co-CEOs
  • Development ends on Hyper Light Breaker as studio Heart Machine says it’s making layoffs - The Hyper Light Drifter prequel has had mixed reviews whilst in Early Access

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-09
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • This year’s Fortnitemares event in Fortnite adds Jason (Friday 13th), Scooby-Doo, Ghostface, Huggy Wuggy from Poppy Playtime and Doja Cat
  • Chucky, Jason Voorhees and Predator are coming to Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 and Warzone

Free Games / Subscription

  • Three Nintendo NSO SNES games are added to NSO on October 10th; Bubsy, Mario & Wario and Fatal Fury Special
  • Xbox reveals October’s Game Pass games

Business

  • Laid-off devs of cancelled ZeniMax MMO Project Blackbird form new studio 'with no outside investors and full creative control,' and they're calling it Sackbird
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 tops 5 million sales worldwide in 5 months

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-08
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Silent Hill f has been added to GOG; one of the "earliest" third party AAA releases the platform has seen to date
  • PowerWash Simulator 2 launches later this month - 23rd October for PC, PS5, Switch 2, Xbox Series S/X

Free Games / Subscription

  • Alan Wake 2 is available on PlayStation Plus

Business

  • Epic sues Fortnite fans who used over 20,000 bots to get paid tens of thousands of dollars - accused of falsifying the popularity of their islands, Epic Games says
  • Breakout hit Megabonk sells more than a million copies on Steam
  • Nintendo is seeking $4.5 million in damages from a Reddit moderator it says widely pirated Switch games

Rumours

  • PlayStation Plus is reportedly adding Silent Hill 2 Remake. If rumour is true, expected to be added October 21st

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-07
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Business

  • Sega’s ‘Sonic the Hedgehog’ Sets Multiyear Licensing Deal With International Olympic Committee

Rumours

item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Free Games / Subscription

Business

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-02
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Xbox Game Pass is increasing in price by 50%
  • Rock Band 4 will be delisted this weekend, with its DLC to follow
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 multiplayer and zombies changes detailed in depth by Treyarch
  • Namco’s first ever video game from 1978 is getting its first re-release this week - Gee Bee
  • Ghost of Yotei’s day one patch improves environmental detail and improves ray tracing
  • Moonlighter 2 delayed to November due to overstuffed October release schedule
  • Man pleads guilty to sending death threats to Epic Games

Business

  • Ubisoft’s Tencent-backed subsidiary starts operation today, is named Vantage Studios
  • Former TT Games boss has launched a new studio to work on a Peter Pan game
  • Dune Awakening developer Funcom suffers layoffs after biggest release in studio history
  • BioWare's future under EA in question, studio veteran warns, if it makes "the kind of games that this new company isn't interested in making anymore"
  • EA says there will be no "immediate changes" to its workforce following leveraged buyout
  • Hasbro opens new Wizards of the Coast development hub in Montreal

Rumours

  • It looks like Prototype could be coming back after a Steam update added new credits - An update to the Steam version of Prototype has added new credits and references to Ubisoft Connect, which could point to a re-release of the game.

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-10-01
item.description:

Business

Rumours

  • Sony is reportedly set to release an updated PS5 DualSense controller with a removable battery

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-30
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Business

item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Business

Business

item.description:

  • PlayStation Store were among the other winners

Free Games / Subscription

Kojima News Round-Up

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-22
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Lenovo has cancelled some pre-orders of its $1,100 Legion Go 2, saying it’s ‘substantially exceeded expectations
  • Microsoft is raising Xbox console prices in the US again, five months after its last price increase - The price of Xbox Series X and S consoles in the US increased by $100 and $80 respectively

Business

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-19
item.description:

Free Games / Subscription

  • Two more Game Boy Advance games are coming to Switch Online’s Nintendo Classics library next week - September 25.

Business

  • Infinity Festival to celebrate Innovators in the Age this October

Game News Round-Up

item.description:

item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Free Games / Subscription

  • September’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue update

Business

  • UK games industry trade body UKIE has partnered with Tencent, the Department for Business and Trade, and delivery partner Player One Consulting, for the second year of its Video Games Growth Programme, designed "to help ambitious studios across the UK scale both creatively and commercially."

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-10
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Beloved Japanese racehorse that inspired Umamusume: Pretty Derby character has passed away - Haru Urara was 29. Over her race career, Haru ran in 113 races, losing every single one of them
  • Codemasters’ old Dirt and Grid titles lose online services on March 16th, 2026 - Games to be affected are Dirt Showdown, Dirt 3, Grid Autosport and Grid 2

Business

  • Overwatch GM Walter Kong steps up as SVP head of development, live games/mobile - Despite the promotion, Kong says he will remain leading Overwatch's "overall strategy" but will be expanding that remit to encompass Hearthstone, Diablo Immortal, and Warcraft Rumble, too
  • Dead Island 2 reaches 20m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-09
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Ready or Not passes 13m copies sold across platforms - 3m on console
  • Roblox kicks off Roblox Developers Conference 2025 with a "suite of innovations," including an 8.5% boost to creator revenue - the company also unveiled its new generation of "fully functional 4D objects with AI"

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-08
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • ‘Skate’ soundtrack confirms Royel Otis, Wallows and more than 90 other names

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-05
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Roblox says all players who communicate in-game will need an age check by the end of the year - The platform is also launching new systems which limit communication between adult and child players

item.description:
Game News Round-Up

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-02
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Kojima Productions to show ‘future project’s during Tokyo Game Show week this month - Celebrating 10 years with an anniversary event. ‘Beyond the Strand’ will take place on Tuesday, September 23 – two days before TGS 2025 – at Tokyo’s Roppongi Hills TOHO Cinema
  • A PlayStation State of Play focusing on 007 First Light is coming this week - September 3 at 11am PT / 7pm BST / 8pm CEST
  • The Cyberpunk 2077 team is seeking "brave netrunners" for something on 4th September, but won't say what it is
  • Left 4 Dead creator Mike Booth has announced he is working on a new four-player co-op shooter
  • Escape from Tarkov is coming to Steam

Business

  • Rare veteran Louise O'Connor lands new role at Xbox Game Studios (Chief of Staff) after Everwild cancellation
  • Daniel Drapeau, recently the game director on Fairgames at Haven Studios, has announced his new role as a creative director over at Warner Bros Montreal - The move adds further doubt to the status of Fairgames, the live-service shooter published by Sony, following the departure of studio founder Jade Raymond
  • Gears of War: Reloaded surpasses 1m players - Gears of War: Ultimate Edition multiplatform remaster launched on August 26, 2025
  • Nintendo rebrands transmedia division from Warpstar to Nintendo Stars - The firm said it was "strengthening" the "ancillary use" of films featuring Nintendo IP to encompass live events, merchandise, and more

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-09-01
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • 8 weeks before release, Mortal Kombat 2 (the movie) has been delayed 7 months - Mortal Kombat 2 will now release on May 15, 2026. Rumored reason is "movies typically don’t perform well at the box office in October"
  • Yooka-Replaylee confirmed for October at $30, additional 30% discount for original owners
  • Steam now requires age verification in the UK for mature game pages
  • Capcom confirms Pragmata isn’t a secret Mega Man game
  • Stardew Valley will be getting another update - no release date given at this time
  • Honkai Nexus Anima revealed, a spin-off creature collector from Genshin Impact studio - Those interested can sign up for the test via the official website, with the recruitment period lasting from now until 12th September
  • F2P RPG Duet Night Abyss abandons gacha mechanics just weeks before launch in response to "consistent" player "dissatisfaction"

Business

  • Curveball Leisure launches full-service publishing division - Curveball Games aims to fund "unique" titles from indie to AA helmed by senior publishing manager Ed Gregory, formerly of PlayStation and PQube
  • CD Projekt H1 profit rises 4.3% to $121m
  • NetEase shuts down Rich Vogel-led T-Minus Zero Entertainment

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-29
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Unreal Engine 5 performance issues are mainly due to devs not optimising properly, Epic CEO Tim Sweeney says - Not enough studios optimise for less powerful hardware early in the development process, he claims
  • Metal Gear Solid Delta Snake Eater issues acknowledged by Konami, patch on the way

Business

  • 75.4% of Cyberpunk 2077’s Nintendo Switch 2 sales were physical
  • Thunderful Group shareholders approve $5.3 million Atari acquisition

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-28
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Side and Razer announce 'human-in-the-loop' AI playtests - The tool uses AI to analyze data from user playtests, and the information is then sent to QA analysts. The companies claim this will lead to 'more testers and more playtest hours' at the 'same or lower cost' than traditional playtests

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox is bringing Cloud Gaming to lower Game Pass tiers, in addition to PC games
  • PlayStation Plus’s September games include a 3D platformer and two indie hits - Psychonauts 2, Stardew Valley and Viewfinder

Business

  • Atari has acquired five Ubisoft games (Child of Eden, Grow Home, Cold Fear, I Am Alive, Grow Up) and will re-release and ‘evolve’ them
  • Crystal Dynamics lays off "a number of talented colleagues" - unclear how many have been affected at this time
  • Square Enix shutters two ten-year-old mobile games in Japan - Final Fantasy Brave Exvius and Dragon Quest of the Stars
  • Part of the team behind EA's cancelled Black Panther game joins Wizards of the Coast - Led by Monolith Productions veteran Michael de Plater, the group of 15 ex-Cliffhanger Games employees will be working on a new title

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-27
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Return to Silent Hill ("Silent Hill 2"-based) Movie gets First Teaser Trailer
  • Iconic game publisher Acclaim marks its return with a showcase next month - Showcase will take place on September 10 at 11.30am PS / 2.30pm ET / 7.30pm BST
  • Skate’s Early Access date has finally been confirmed - September 16 for PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S / X, PC

Business

  • PlayStation CEO doesn't want first party studios to "play it safe" but "fail early and cheaply"
  • New games industry body launches in India - Indian Game Publishers and Developers Association (IGPDA) was founded by nine companies, including Nazara Technologies and Tara Gaming
  • Developer Rec Room lays off 'about half' its staff
  • Evil Empire tells devs to avoid early access unless their project is 90 percent complete

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-26
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Dead or Alive 6 director is leaving Bandai Namco, after working on Tekken 8
  • The Division 2: Survivors has been announced, coming in 2026 - Survivors is described as a "Survival Extraction" experience
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live 2025 audience reaches over 72m viewers

Business

  • 357,000 visitors attended Gamescom 2025 - More than 34,000 trade visitors and 1,568 exhibitors participated in the event
  • Devcom 2025 attendance up 6% from 2024 - Conference featured 390 speakers, a rise of 30% from the previous year

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-22
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection adds the lowest rated game in franchise history - Mortal Kombat Mythologies: Sub Zero and Mortal Kombat: Special Forces
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong release date announced - September 4, 2025 for PS4/PS5, Xbox One / Series S/X, Nintendo Switch / Switch 2

Business

  • Devcom to rebrand as Gamescom Dev
  • Pete Parsons leaves Bungie, Justin Truman steps in as CEO

Rumours

  • The setting for the next Forza Horizon game has seemingly leaked via a car company - If true, Forza Horizon's next entry would be based in Japan

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-21
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Xbox ROG Ally line coming in October with Handheld Compatibility Program - Both handhelds will be released on October 16. Details about pricing and pre-orders are coming soon, according to Microsoft.
  • Halloween Revealed by Friday the 13th: The Game Developer Illfonic - Includes Single Player Mode
  • Infinity Nikki Is Getting a Stardew Valley Collab in September (September 1st)
  • Final Fantasy 14's Yoshi-P Says There May Be 'Good News' in the Future About FF14 on Nintendo Switch 2

Business

  • Unknown Worlds sues former leadership team for breach of employment and "fiduciary duty of care" - Founders are also accused of downloading tens of thousands of company documents before their termination
  • Peak Sells 10 Million Copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-20
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Black Forest Games producer Dennis Schiefer has passed away
  • Second season of Fallout TV series to premiere on December 17, 2025 - New trailer showcases New Vegas and Deathclaw
  • Crunchyroll reveals first footage of Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice anime - Sekiro: No Defeat releases next year
  • Remedy launches new streaming series "looking back on the games we made" - Series kicks off with Control on August 27
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 is getting a four-player co-op campaign and more - Multiplayer and zombies news is coming at Call of Duty Next on September 30th. Game releases November 14th
  • Helldivers 2 x Halo ODST Legendary warbond releases August 26th
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong announcent due Thursday 21st August (7:30 AM PT | 10:30 AM ET | 3:30 PM UK)
  • Vampire: The Masquerade Bloodlines 2 finally gets a release date, locks two of its clans behind $22 DLC
  • Wukong follow-up Black Myth: Zhong Kui revealed at Gamescom
  • Ghost of Yotei is getting Legends multiplayer mode next year
  • Monster Hunter Wilds and Final Fantasy 14 are officially crossing over - Both games will get content to celebrate
  • Monkey Island and Maniac Mansion veteran Ron Gilbert shows his next game, Death by Scrolling
  • Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 has a new developer (King Art Games), promises ‘return to roots’
  • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 next year
  • TT Games reveals Skywalker Saga-style Lego Batman game, Legacy of the Dark Knight - Features Matt Berry as Bane
  • Sonic and Pac-Man’s next games will have crossover DLC featuring each other
  • Atari is releasing a brand new Bubsy game; Bubsy 4D

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass August line-up

Business

  • Starbreeze says PUBG collab was its 'single largest revenue contributor' in Q2
  • Cloud Chamber lays off "unspecified" number of staff as Rod Fergusson is parachuted in to lead troubled BioShock 4 production

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-19
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • A new Nintendo Direct focused on Kirby Air Riders is happening today - 8am PT / 11am ET / 2pm BST
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live today - 7pm BST
  • Shenmue 3 is getting an Enhanced edition, including Xbox and Nintendo versions for the first time - PS5, Xbox Series S/X, PC and "Nintendo"

Business

  • Puzzle & Dragons studio says a former executive embezzled $2.3 million of company funds - Gungho's already under-fire CEO will take a pay cut for three months
  • Peak sold 1.7m copies last week

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-18
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • EA's upcoming Skate will have 100 songs on its soundtrack for early access (full song list TBD)

Business

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-15
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Business

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue for August

Business

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-13
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • This year’s Gamescom will have more than 1,500 exhibitors for the first time - The show floor will also take up a record high of 233,000 square metres
  • Senua’s Saga: Hellblade 2 Enhanced out now on PS5, with a free update for Xbox and PC

Business

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-12
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Microsoft will "continue to support Forza Motorsport and Forza Horizon 5," despite Turn 10 cuts

Business

  • Sega Q1 Results

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-11
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Tencent quietly updates "slavish Horizon clone" Light of Motiram's Steam page; removed, edited, and/or replaced key art, just days after Sony filed a copyright lawsuit

Business

  • Diablo series head Rod Fergusson leaves Blizzard after five years
  • Bithell Games is making ‘the majority’ of its full-time staff redundant - 11 staff have been impacted

Rumours

  • A Far Cry TV show that "doesn't shy away from the darkest, most absurd parts of humanity" is reportedly in development at FX - Ubisoft may have prematurely posted about the project on its website

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-08
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Microsoft has stopped development on Avalanche Studios’ Contraband
  • Sony CFO says Marathon is expected to release before March, and Bungie’s independence is ‘getting lighter’ with the eventual aim to completely intergrate into PlayStation Studios
  • Digital Foundry Leaves IGN (who owns Eurogamer), Now Fully Independent
  • Hexen + Heretic by Nightdive Studios shadowdropped for PC (Steam / GoG), Switch, Xbox Game Pass and PlayStation

Business

  • Sony Vice President, Sadahiko Hayakawa: "We are moving away from a hardware centric business model to a platform business that expands the community and increases engagement."
  • Sony CFO says its live service shift is ‘not entirely going smoothly’ but pledges to carry on and learn from mistakes
  • Sony's PlayStation 5 has sold 80.3 million units as it closes on in on fifth anniversary
  • Konami stocks hit record highs thanks to eFootball and new Momotaro Dentetsu game. The Momotaro Dentetsu series is one of the company's strongest IPs, with sales totalling 18.9 million, almost double that of its horror franchise, Silent Hill, which has sold 11.7m copies since it launched in March 1999
  • Take Two Financial Numbers (for the three months ending June 30, 2025)
  • Sony has released its financial results for Q1 2025 (for the three months ended June 30th, 2025)
  • Unity Financial Results

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-07
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Obituary: Marvel's Spider-Man 2 accessibility engineer Patrick "Thor" Gass has passed away - Gass, a US Army veteran, previously worked at Raven Software as a UI engineer on the Call of Duty series
  • Video Game History Foundation expands its preservation efforts by making "one of the first American" games magazines available for free; Computer Entertainer

Business

  • RedOctane Games is a new studio dedicated to "advancing the rhythm game genre" - Founded by seasoned developers from the rhythm gaming world, who have worked on Guitar Hero and DJ Hero, alongside new talent, RedOctane Games is "focused solely on advancing the rhythm game genre" while "combining the past with the future."
  • Vintage Story developers claim itch.io has withheld payments for three months

Rumours

  • Microsoft Has Reportedly Sold Less Than 30 Million Consoles This Generation (Xbox Series S and X)

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-06
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Fortnite Chapter 6 Season 4 trailer reveals Power Rangers and Halo collaboration - Chapter 6 Season 4 starts August 7th and is called Shock 'n Awesome
  • Nintendo will hold an Indie World live stream on Thursday, August 7 at 6am PT / 9pm ET / 2pm BST / 3pm CEST
  • Peak developers would rather you pirate its game than play Roblox "microtransaction-riddled ripoff slop" called Cliff
  • Genshin Impact PS4 is being discontinued - users are being urged to "move on" to Genshin Impact PS5

Free Games / Subscription

  • August’s PlayStation Plus Essential titles are now available; Lies of P, Day Z and My Hero One's Justice 2
  • Xbox Game Pass titles for August

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-05
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Evercade is getting a 12-game Rare compilation - The cartridge, which is priced at £19.99 / $29.99, will consist of 12 games mainly covering the studio’s early years, including some games released under its previous incarnation Ultimate: Play the Game. Rare Colllection 1 releases in November and is priced at £19.99 / $29.99
  • Tekken 8’s final Season 2 DLC fighter is Miary Zo, a brand new character from Madagascar
  • Mastercard insists it has not "required restrictions of any" game on Steam or itch.io, but Valve claims otherwise
  • One million people claim GOG's FreedomToBuy games in protest of ongoing NSFW game censorship row

Business

  • Call of Duty workers at Raven secure first union contract with Microsoft - Three years after Activision Blizzard first officially recognized the union, the Game Workers Alliance

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-04
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • GUILTY GEAR -STRIVE- Season Pass 4's Lucy (from Cyberpunk Edgerunners) overview trailer released - releases August 21st
  • New VIRTUA FIGHTER project video released showcasing combat
  • Armor King showcased for Tekken 8 Season 2 DLC - Launches October 13th for Season 2 Character Pass owners and October 16th for all users
  • MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls PS5 Closed Beta announced - September 5th - 7th
  • Battlefield 6's UGC is powered by Godot
  • Gothic Classic (Gothic 1,2 and 3) announced for Xbox One / Series S / X, PS4/5

Business

  • BioShock 4’s leadership replaced as 2K insists it’s ‘committed to delivering a great game’ - director and studio head affected after the game reportedly failed an internal development review

Rumours

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-08-01
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Namco’s 2002 platformer Pac-Man World 2 is getting a remake - Pac-Man World 2 Re-Pac is coming to Switch/2, PS4/5, Xbox Series S/X and PC on September 26
  • A brand new Katamari game is coming to PC and consoles in October - Once Upon A Katamari is the first new non-mobile Katamari game in 14 years. Switch, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC on October 24 as a digital-only release
  • A new HD-2D RPG from Square Enix (The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales) has been announced for Switch 2
  • Square Enix announces Octopath Traveler 0 - set for release on Switch and Switch 2 on December 4
  • Monster Hunter Stories 3 announced by Capcom, is coming to Switch 2 next year
  • Bloober's The Medium will be adapted to film, courtesy of It and Annabelle writer-producer Gary Dauberman

Business

  • Nintendo Switch 2 has sold 5.82 million units in Q1 and 8.67 million games in total. Mario Kart World sold nearly as many copies as the console - 5.63 million units
  • Xbox content and services revenue up 13% year-on-year
  • Capcom Q1 Results
  • Roblox Q1 Results

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-31
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The next Nintendo Direct Partner Showcase happens today - 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm BST
  • Pokémon Company removes two TCG Pocket card designs and ‘deeply apologises’ following claims of plagiarism

Business

  • Ready or Not tops 2m console sales in 2 weeks
  • UK game tech company JECO secures $1.3 million in pre-seed investment - Simon Barratt, JECO describes itself as an "industry first" B2B tech toolkit which allows developers "to increase performance whilst reducing production costs, timelines, and carbon consumption by adopting an automated suite of optimization tools for all software, on any device."

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-30
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Mario Kart World’s latest update makes CPU opponents easier - The version 1.2.0 patch also tells players when they've collected everything in Free Roam mode
  • EA CEO says it’s ‘all in as a company’ on Battlefield 6, is building a platform rather than a product

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games for August 5th - Lies of P, Day Z and My Hero One's Justice 2

Business

  • Atari has agreed to acquire Thunderful for around €4.5 million - Atari will own around 82% of Thunderful shares, subject to shareholder approval next month
  • Warner Bros. is hiring (for what appears to be) another live service game
  • Guerrilla's Horizon franchise has sold over 38m copies worldwide
  • IGN launches Gaming Trends platform, shedding light on how mobile, Roblox, and more are reshaping the industry - The initial 2025 Gaming Trends report focuses on six key areas, including mobile gaming, user-generated content, and the trend for nostalgia
  • Krafton reports record revenue, buoyed by PUBG and Inzoi

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-29
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Sony’s PlayStation 5 fight stick is called FlexStrike, is coming in 2026
  • Xbox just leaked Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection's release date - According to the now removed dates on the Xbox App, Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection releases September 29th / September 30th (depending on location in the world)

Free Games / Subscription

  • Mario Paint has been added to Switch Online’s SNES library, supports Switch 2 mouse controls - Switch owners can also play by plugging in a USB Mouse

Business

  • The publisher and developer of mobile hit Puzzle & Dragons (GungHo) says it’s considering a shareholder request for an extraordinary general meeting to oust company president and CEO Kazuki Morishita - Angry shareholders previously called out GungHo for increasing its president's pay whilst profits plummet
  • Sony sues Tencent for "slavish clone" of its "valuable" Horizon franchise - Sony claims Tencent asked to license the IP and when refused, pressed ahead with a "knock-off" version of its own
  • Free-to-play FIFA-licensed mobile game (FIFA Rivals) tops 1m downloads since its launch last month - 5m matches, 16m goals

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-28
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Wolfenstein is the next game series to get an adaptation for TV by Amazon MGM Studios
  • Team Fortress 2's summer update is here, including 10 new community-created maps, the summer 2025 Cosmetic Case - which contains 23 new community-contributed items - four new community-contributed taunts, and 14 new community-created Unusual effects
  • Weeks before release, Dying Light: The Beast has been delayed - The spin-off will now release on September 19, instead of August 22, the developer announced on Friday

Business

  • The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion Remastered has welcomed nine million players since its release
  • Krafton acquires Last Epoch studio, Eleventh Hour Games
  • Former DreadXP and Critical Reflex alum Ted Hentschke has teamed up with Jakub Bałuszek-Sobolewski to form Black Lantern Collective, a new publisher dedicated to horror games

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-25
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The Final Fantasy Tactics remake has been rebuilt from scratch due to source code being overwritten - Square Enix has confirmed that the original code for Final Fantasy Tactics no longer exists
  • Pokémon Trading Card Pocket gets new Johto-themed packs alongside trading update
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 6LP vinyl box set, 8 CD set, and a double LP announced by Laced Records
  • Like Steam before it, Itch.io has hidden its entire NSFW games library, as payment providers pressure stores
  • Lego Game Boy finally revealed after months of leaks - releases October 1, cost $59.99, and will include 421 pieces. Set includes two Lego Game Boy cartridges, one for The Legend of Zelda: Link’s Awakening, and one for Super Mario Land

Business

  • Elden Ring Nightreign has sold 5 million units, Shadow of the Erdtree 10 million
  • Hasbro's recent Magic the Gathering Final Fantasy crossover card set made $200m sales in one day
  • Sony has acquired 2.5% of Elden Ring publisher Bandai Namco in a ‘strategic partnership’
  • Nintendo Switch 2 set a US launch month record at 1.6 million units - 82% of Nintendo Switch 2 buyer during June also acquired Mario Kart World

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-24
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Bethesda veteran and 'father of the Elder Scrolls' Julian LeFay dies, aged 59 - Following news earlier in the week that he left the games industry due to cancer
  • Sony’s new Power Saver mode will ‘scale back performance’ of supported PlayStation 5 titles - Alongside the Power Save mode, the latest beta firmware will also allow players to pair a DualSense controller with up to 4 devices at once
  • Microsoft has reversed plans to charge $80 for The Outer Worlds 2 - the Obisidian sequel will now launch for $70 due to "current market conditions" according to Microsoft

Business

  • Scottish Silent Hill developer NoCode has changed its name to Screen Burn: ‘We have coders now’
  • Fortnite's return to iOS in the UK "uncertain", as Epic accuses UK regulator of deprioritising store competition
  • Sandsoft shutters two internal studios, impacting 65 jobs in Riyadh and Barcelona
  • Rematch reaches five million players one month after launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-23
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • EA confirms it will reveal Battlefield 6 this week - reveal trailer July 24th
  • Pokémon Champions: New details revealed for Switch and mobile battling game that is similar to Pokémon Stadium
  • Saber Interactive is working on a single-player Hellraiser game for PS5, Xbox, and PC
  • Surprise Switch / mobile game Pokémon Friends is out now - "Plush-filled puzzle game packed with charming challenges"
  • Splitgate 2 ‘unlaunches,’ studio to cut staff ahead of 2026 rerelease - Suffered from 96% player drop. This is the second round of layoffs since June with an unknown number of employees affected. Game is going back to Beta
  • Critical Role working on its first video game, which will be set in Exandria - Dispatch is currently set to release later in 2025

Business

  • Supermassive has delayed Directive 8020 amid a round of layoffs - around 36 employees affected
  • Ubisoft to restructure as "Creative Houses" as Q1 25-26 financials come in "lower-than-expected"
  • Side, a support studio that has lent services to games like the Silent Hill 2 remake, Kingdom Come Deliverance II, and Baldur's Gate 3, has laid off 'less than 10' staff

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-22
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Tomb Raider composer faces 16 months in jail over Covid loan fraud - Peter Connelly provided music for Tomb Raider: The Last Revelation, Chronicles, and The Angel of Darkness
  • Atari announces special console and Atari 50 DLC for Pac-Man’s anniversary - DLC includes Pac-Man 2600, Galaga, Dig Dug, Xevious, Galaxian, and more
  • The Pokémon Company will hold a Pokémon Presents presentation today - 6 a.m. PT / 9 a.m. ET / 2pm UK Time

Business

  • Still Wakes the Deep studio The Chinese Room regains independence, ‘working on 2 original IPs’ - Layoffs were confirmed as part of its split from Sumo Digital

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-21
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • 12 years later, Grand Theft Auto V has been cleared for release in Saudi Arabia - made possible in part due to the introduction of a 21+ age rating
  • Microsoft quietly removes ability to purchase movies and TV shows on Xbox and the Microsoft Store - previous purchased content still viewable for now
  • Bethesda veteran Julian LeFay, 'father of The Elder Scrolls', departs game development due to cancer
  • Smartphone racer Mario Kart Tour gets first new content in years to celebrate Switch 2's Mario Kart World

Business

  • Ready or Not hits 1m console sales in under four days

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-18
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • The Mortal Kombat 2 movie trailer is here, featuring Karl Urban’s Johnny Cage - Film due for release October 24th
  • Nintendo is doing another test via the "Nintendo Switch Online Playtest Program" - starts on July 21st and is run on a first-come-first-served system. Wording is unclear but it seems to be a playtest of the same experience from October last year
  • YouTuber raided by police for reviewing retro gaming handhelds - Once Were Nerd faces a maximum fine of around $17,000 and up to three years in prison. The issue stems from promoting (via review, not from endorsement) "retro" devices that contain thousands of game roms
  • Ubisoft and Netflix greenlight Assassin's Creed live-action series
  • FFXIV to stop support for Windows 10 on October 14, 2025. This is due to Microsoft ending security updates for the OS. The game will still boot and play on Windows 10, though it will no longer be an officially supported version
  • Saber Interactive Is Teasing Something With Spooky Vibes For Later This Month - Online consensus believes the game to be Hellraiser related

Free Games / Subscription

  • Civilization 6 Premium Edition is currently free to claim on the Epic Games Store

Business

  • Former German development studio Artex brought back to life as a publisher after 25 years - shift from developing games to supporting "young studios and start-ups"
  • Co-dev specialists Virtuos announces layoffs affecting 7% of workforce - Around 270 employees in Asia and Europe are affected
  • PlayStation Antitrust Settlement Rejected Over Lack of Clarity

Rumours

  • Marvel's Blade budget is 94m euros according to France's Committee of Finance

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-17
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • 7 months after Cyberpunk 2077’s ‘last’ update, yet more new content has been revealed - AutoDrive, self-driving Delamain cabs, Mac port and more
  • Sam Lake and Laura Bailey join cast of Surgent Studios’ Dead Take - Cast includes Matthew Mercer, Travis Willingham, CDawgVA, Jane Perry, Alanah Pearce, Ben Starr and Neil Newbon
  • Zelda and Link’s movie actors revealed for The Legend of Zelda live-action movie; 16-year-old British actor Benjamin Evan Ainsworth, best known as the voice of Pinocchio in Disney’s live-action remake, will take on the role of Link. Zelda will be played by 21-year-old English actor Bo Bragason, who is known for her roles in the BBC One series Three Girls and The Jetty, and the Disney+ series Renegade Nell
  • Pokémon TCG Pocket finally receives much-needed trading overhaul - update will take place on 29th July

Business

  • Ubisoft names Yves Guillemot’s son Charlie Guillemot as co-CEO of new Tencent-backed subsidiary
  • Steam introduces vague new rules banning 'certain kinds of adult content' to appease credit card and payment processor companies - Online feedback has been extremely negative with people pointing out banks should not be able to dictate what games a person should be allowed / not allowed to purchase and play
  • Metaphor: ReFantazio has sold 2m units worldwide, Atlus officially reveals
  • Sega, Netflix and others will let Roblox creators monetize their major franchises

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-16
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • John Wick Hex is being delisted (removed from sale) on July 17th
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 dev says it’s removing a cheeky Guitar Hero reference and ‘investigating’ how it happened - Easter egg was a Guitar Hero World Tour-style box was placed prominently in a bin. Neversoft (developers of classic THPS) were the developers of Guitar Hero World Tour. Fans have taken the easter egg as a mean spirited jab at Neversoft
  • Free-to-play MOBA Predecessor follows Fortnite with return to original Paragon map Agora
  • Cyberpunk 2077's delayed 2.3 update is being revealed today
  • Uma Musume: Pretty Derby players are sending massive amounts of ryegrass to a retired Japanese race horse. Haru Urara, depicted as a lighthearted pink-haired horse girl in Uma Musame: Pretty Derby, didn't win a single race IRL, which is reflected in-game with career goals focused on gaining fans rather than taking home trophies. The character in-game has quickly become a fan-favourite and fans have been showing their appreciation by sending the real horse ryegrass

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for July

Business

  • Epic Games confirms legal action against Fortnite cheat seller and DDoS cyber attacker:

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-15
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • FBI says it's now seized "multiple" ROM piracy sites, claims downloads resulted in $170m losses in just three months
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 locks SpongeBob and TMNT Easter Eggs behind $70 Deluxe Edition
  • Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War Definitive Edition and Space Marine 1 re-release coming this year

Business

  • Way of the Samurai studio Acquire Corp and Red Dunes Games announce collaboration on multiple new IPs
  • Xbox graphics department lead puts up hiring post on LinkedIn, uses AI graphics in post

Rumours

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-14
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Summer Games Done Quick 2025 raises £1.8m for Doctors Without Borders

Business

  • Lords of the Fallen surpasses 5.5m players

Rumours

  • Forza Horizon 5 Sold 2 Million Units in One Month on PS5
  • An untitled Donkey Kong film has been recently registered at the U.S Copyright Office by Universal Pictures and Nintendo

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-11
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • GameStop is auctioning off the staple and stapler that damaged a customer’s Nintendo Switch 2 - the damaged Switch 2, which Gamestop has repaired, is also part of the auction
  • Sea of Thieves is getting multi-ship private servers and paid custom servers "early" next year
  • Nintendo is discontinuing Switch Game Vouchers - Existing vouchers will continue to work after the termination date in early 2026

Business

  • SAG-AFTRA approves 2025 Video Game Agreement, officially concluding its strike
  • Krafton addresses leadership changes at Unknown Worlds, former executives file lawsuit against publisher - Firm claims "absence of core leadership resulted in repeated confusion in direction" resulting in delay of Subnautica 2

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-10
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Astro Bot’s award winning soundtrack is coming soon to vinyl, and it’s available for pre-order now
  • Stalker 2 is coming to PS5 later this year with PS5 Pro enhancements
  • Limited Run’s SNES release of Doom is available for pre-order now, alongside a new SNES controller - This version of the game includes “impossible graphics tricks”, according to Limited Run, as well as levels missing from the original game. Other new features include support for circle-strafing and the ability for monsters to respawn on Nightmare difficulty. Controller comes with rumble support and Limited Run is open-sourcing support for it
  • Dead by Daylight details its Walking Dead collaboration as public test build goes live
  • Live-service mech shooter Steel Hunters will shut down in October, announced just three months after it entered early access

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus games for July

Business

  • UK Video Games Council confirms inaugural members - The new organization will "play a vital role in representing the views of the sector" to UK government

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-09
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • You can now play The Last of Us Part 2 in yet another way, as Naughty Dog releases free "Chronological" mode
  • A Ghost of Yotei State of Play featuring ‘nearly 20 minutes’ of gameplay is coming this week - July 10 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm BST / 11pm CEST
  • Capcom cancels Monster Hunter lecture as ‘staff face death threats from players’ over the game’s performance issues - unfortunately the talk was titled "Monster Hunter: Wilds runs smoothly! Everything you need to know about optimization"
  • Animal Crossing Crocs collaboration announced - releases 26th August

Free Games / Subscription

  • XCOM dev’s highly rated Marvel’s Midnight Suns is ‘free’ to claim for a limited time for Prime Gaming users

Business

  • Epic Games settles antitrust lawsuit with Samsung - "We are grateful that Samsung will address Epic’s concerns," said Epic CEO

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-08
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Unity promises strong AI copyright 'guardrails' after employee conjures Mickey Mouse on stream
  • Stop Killing Games' proposals for game preservation "prohibitively expensive", major publishers insist - as EU petition surpasses 1m signatures
  • Valve blocks mod on Mount and Blade: Warband in South Korea following request from government - Mod was a "modern depiction of the Gwangju Democratisation Movement" that "distorted historical facts", says South Korean government

Business

  • Romero Games disputes claims it has "completely closed" - The studio says it is "doing everything in our power" to remain open
  • Square Enix has invested in a Danish indie studio BetaDwarf to fund its ‘dream game’ Vault Breakers - Publisher says it is 'always looking' for exciting indie titles to support

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-07
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Cyberpunk: Edgerunners is officially getting a second season
  • A Resident Evil mobile strategy game, Resident Evil Survival Unit, will be revealed July 10th - co-developed by Joycity (3on3 FreeStyle Rebound) and Aniplex (Demon Slayer -The Hinokami Chronicles 2) for Capcom. Kingdom Hearts’ co-creator Shinji Hashimoto is involved
  • Destiny: Rising, the free-to-play mobile version of Bungie's long-running sci-fi shooter series, has been given a release date; 28th August for iOS and Android - Collaboration between Bungie and NetEase, described as a "shared world" adventure set in an "alternate Destiny timeline, exploring a narrative in the post-Dark Age era"

Business

  • Double Eleven sister studio Cast Iron Games has opened its doors in Wakefield - focusing on co-development and porting
  • Stockholm-based Toca Boca lays off unknown number of employees

Rumours

  • Around 300 Build A Rocket Boy employees reportedly receive "at-risk of redundancy" emails following layoffs announcement

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-04
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • PlayStation hit Helldivers 2 is officially coming to Xbox with crossplay with all three platforms - releases August 26th
  • EA to sunset Anthem in January 2026 - Devs NOT affected by layoffs

Business

  • John Romero’s next FPS has lost funding in the wake of the Microsoft cuts - A Romero Games employee has claimed the “whole studio” is being let go, with many advertising on LinkedIn that they’ve lost their jobs
  • ZeniMax Online Studios head Matt Firor to depart after 18 years
  • G/O Media sells Kotaku to digital publishing company Keleops - G/O Media winding down operations following Keleops acquisition of Kotaku

Rumours

  • Dying Light developer Techland has reportedly cancelled two games - losses of 130 million Polish złoty (approximately 37.3 million USD)
  • 2K mobile studio Cat Daddy Games is making layoffs

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-03
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Neil Druckmann is stepping away from The Last of Us TV show to ‘transition complete focus’ on game development
  • Blizzard is giving up on its Warcraft mobile game (Warcraft Rumble) amid layoffs - will no longer be developing new content for the free-to-play mobile strategy game, and instead focus on "regular, systemic in-game events and bug fixes."
  • Nexus Mods is getting age verification for UK and EU users, weeks after changing owners
  • Rockstar releases surprise Red Dead Online update, despite previously ending major support - The update is known as Strange Tales of the West, includes four missions based on supernatural elements
  • Dave the Diver director says there’s ‘nothing indie’ about it, and he didn’t apply for its controversial Game Awards nod
  • Mark Cerny says PS5 Pro will get its major ‘replacement for the current PSSR’ upscaler next year - AMD's FSR 4 will replace PSSR

Business

  • ZeniMax Online Studios boss confirms departure amidst rumours of Blackbird project cancellation
  • Xbox closes The Initiative and cancels its Perfect Dark reboot
  • Three former Ubisoft execs receive suspended prison sentences at harassment trial
  • 9,100+ employees affected by layoffs, studio closures and project cancellations across Microsoft
  • Rare's Everwild has been cancelled

Rumours

  • Banjo-Kazooie, Sea of Thieves director, Gregg Mayles, leaves Rare after 35 years, following Everwild cancellation
  • Return to Moria developer Free Range Games lays off 80 people - entire development team

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-02
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

Free Games / Subscription

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-07-01
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • Hideo Kojima says he personally inserted all his "Hideo" cameos in Death Stranding 2 - Kojima has joked that his staff pretend not to know what he’s asking them to do when it comes to "Kojima" references, so he adds them himself

Business

  • Don't Nod confirms layoffs at its Montreal studio
  • FTC reopens refunds for players affected by unwanted purchases in Fortnite - Agency issued over $126 million in compensation last week, deadline for submitting a claim is July 9, 2025

Rumours

  • Black Shamrock laid off 11 employees

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-06-30
item.description:
Game News Round-Up

  • A Short Hike dev halts work on unfinished RPG, saying, "I might never finish this, so I'm releasing the demo!"

  • National Videogame Museum to feature at World Expo 2025

Free Games / Subscription

Business

  • Obituary: Unity senior program manager Andy Brammall has passed away

item.description:

Business

  • Game Dev Heroes co-founder Alex Boucher awarded MBE
  • FBC: Firebreak surpasses 1m players - Remedy's first self published co-op title launched on June 17, 2025
  • Square "collapsed" after Final Fantasy creator Hironobu Sakaguchi left, says composer Nobuo Uematsu
  • Steam's fourth most played game [Bongo Cat] doesn't really make any money, and its developer says it's better off that way
  • Sonic joins McLaren Racing in historic partnership with Sega

item.title: Game News Round-Up — 2025-06-26
item.description:
Game News Round-Up
